introduction: This specification defines the `workspaces:write` and `workspaces:delete` permission scopes, the workspace update endpoint (`PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/:slug`), two new workspace metadata fields (`display_name`, `description`), a corresponding CLI command, and the reference documentation for the full af-hub workspace API and CLI surface. Note: requirements referencing `apikit` APIs (e.g., `apikit.Permission`) are based on unverified PRD assumptions and should be cross-checked against the installed library version before implementation.

glossary.MountHandlers: The Go function that registers all HTTP routes and permission scopes for the af-hub service
glossary.PAT: Personal Access Token — a scoped credential issued to a user for delegated API access
glossary.PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/:slug: The new REST endpoint for updating mutable properties of a single workspace identified by its slug
glossary.POST /api/v1/workspaces: Existing REST endpoint for creating a new workspace
glossary.afc: The af-hub command-line interface binary used by end users to interact with the hub API
glossary.afc workspace create: CLI subcommand that calls `POST /api/v1/workspaces` to create a new workspace
glossary.afc workspace update: CLI subcommand that calls `PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/:slug` to update mutable workspace properties
glossary.anti-enumeration: The practice of returning 404 instead of 403 when a requester lacks access, to avoid leaking the existence of a resource
glossary.apikit: The `github.com/txsvc/apikit` Go library used for PAT permission registration, authentication, and standard JSON error envelopes
glossary.apikit.Permission: The apikit function/type used to register a named permission scope in the PAT permission registry within `MountHandlers`
glossary.description: Optional free-form text describing a workspace's purpose; defaults to empty string when omitted or cleared
glossary.display_name: Optional human-readable label for a workspace; defaults to the slug value when omitted or cleared
glossary.docs/api.md: The REST API reference documentation file covering all af-hub endpoints
glossary.docs/cli.md: The CLI reference documentation file covering all `afc` commands and flags
glossary.org_id: UUID foreign key associating a workspace with an organization; nullable
glossary.owner_id: UUID identifying the user who owns the workspace
glossary.slug: The immutable primary key and globally unique URL-safe identifier for a workspace (e.g., `my-project`)
glossary.status: Workspace lifecycle state; one of `active` or `archived`
glossary.updated_at: RFC 3339 timestamp column updated to the current server time on every successful workspace mutation
glossary.workspaces table: The SQLite/SQL table storing workspace rows, including the new `display_name` and `description` columns
glossary.workspaces:create: Existing PAT permission scope that grants workspace creation and implies read access
glossary.workspaces:delete: PAT permission scope that grants deletion of archived workspaces owned by the PAT's user
glossary.workspaces:read: Existing PAT permission scope that grants list and view access to the PAT owner's workspaces
glossary.workspaces:write: PAT permission scope that grants archive, reactivate, and property-update access to the PAT owner's workspaces
glossary.branch: Optional git ref stored on a workspace; nullable. Immutable after creation.
glossary.git_url: HTTPS or SSH URL of the git repository associated with a workspace. Immutable after creation.
glossary.created_at: RFC 3339 timestamp recording when the workspace was created; immutable after creation
glossary.archived: Workspace lifecycle status indicating the workspace is read-only; all state preserved, reversible via reactivation
glossary.--display-name: CLI flag for `afc workspace update` and `afc workspace create` that sets the workspace display_name
glossary.--description: CLI flag for `afc workspace update` and `afc workspace create` that sets the workspace description
glossary.--org: CLI flag that accepts an organization slug for workspace association
glossary.--clear-display-name: CLI flag for `afc workspace update` that resets display_name to the server-side default (slug value)
glossary.--clear-description: CLI flag for `afc workspace update` that resets description to the server-side default (empty string)
glossary.--clear-org: CLI flag for `afc workspace update` that removes the organization association (sets org_id to null)
glossary.<slug>: Positional CLI argument representing the workspace slug to operate on
glossary.display_name: null: JSON value in a PATCH body that clears display_name to the server-side default
glossary.description: null: JSON value in a PATCH body that clears description to the server-side default
glossary.org_id: null: JSON value in a PATCH body that removes the organization association

requirements.title: New workspace metadata fields: display_name and description

user_story.goal: attach a human-readable name and description to a workspace at creation and update time
user_story.benefit: workspaces are identifiable and self-documenting without relying solely on the slug

acceptance_criteria.action: includes `display_name` (TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '') and `description` (TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '') columns in the `workspaces table` DDL
sets `display_name` to the workspace `slug` value
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: workspace response object contains `display_name` equal to the slug string

acceptance_criteria.action: sets `description` to empty string
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: workspace response object contains `description` equal to empty string

acceptance_criteria.action: includes `display_name` and `description` as non-null strings in every workspace response object (create, list, get, update, archive, reactivate)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: JSON response fields `display_name` and `description` are always present strings, never null

acceptance_criteria.action: stores and returns the provided value as `display_name`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: workspace response `display_name` equals the submitted string
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `display_name` is provided at creation with a non-empty value up to 128 characters

acceptance_criteria.action: stores and returns the provided value as `description`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: workspace response `description` equals the submitted string
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `description` is provided at creation with a non-empty value up to 1024 characters

edge_cases.return_contract: returns HTTP 400 with JSON error body {error: string}
returns HTTP 400 with JSON error body {error: string}

edge_cases.action: normalizes `display_name` to the `slug` value rather than storing null or empty string
edge_cases.return_contract: workspace response `display_name` equals the slug string
edge_cases.trigger: `display_name` is set to null or empty string at creation

edge_cases.action: normalizes `description` to empty string rather than storing null
edge_cases.trigger: `description` is set to null at creation

requirements.title: Register workspaces:write and workspaces:delete permission scopes

user_story.benefit: PATs can be issued with fine-grained delegation of mutation and deletion capabilities without granting full owner-level access

acceptance_criteria.action: registers `workspaces:write` with `apikit.Permission` alongside the existing `workspaces:read` and `workspaces:create` scopes
registers `workspaces:delete` with `apikit.Permission` alongside the existing scopes
leaves `workspaces:read` and `workspaces:create` scopes and their behavior unchanged after adding the two new scopes
treats `workspaces:write` as implying read access to the `PAT` owner's own workspaces (list and get)
does NOT grant read access to a `PAT` holding only `workspaces:delete`; the caller must supply the `slug` directly or also hold `workspaces:read`

edge_cases.action: denies the list/get request using anti-enumeration
edge_cases.return_contract: returns HTTP 404 with JSON error body {error: string}
edge_cases.error_condition: a `PAT` with `workspaces:delete` attempts to list or get workspaces without also holding `workspaces:read` or `workspaces:write`

requirements.title: Access control matrix enforcement for all workspace endpoints

user_story.goal: have each workspace endpoint enforce the correct permission scope per the updated access control matrix
user_story.benefit: permissions are least-privilege and prevent unauthorized mutations or deletions

acceptance_criteria.action: allows admin tokens to call update, archive, reactivate, and delete endpoints on any workspace
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 200 on success for each operation

acceptance_criteria.action: allows workspace owners using their own API key to call update, archive, reactivate, and delete on their own workspaces
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 200 on success for each operation

acceptance_criteria.action: allows a `PAT` with `workspaces:write` to call update, archive, and reactivate on workspaces owned by the `PAT`'s user
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 200 on success

acceptance_criteria.action: denies the request using anti-enumeration
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 404 with JSON error body {error: string}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: a `PAT` with `workspaces:write` attempts to delete a workspace

acceptance_criteria.action: allows a `PAT` with `workspaces:delete` to delete an archived workspace owned by the `PAT`'s user
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 200 on success

acceptance_criteria.action: denies the request using anti-enumeration
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 404 with JSON error body {error: string}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: a `PAT` with `workspaces:read` or `workspaces:create` attempts to call update, archive, reactivate, or delete

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request — workspace creation requires a real user as owner
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 403 with JSON error body {error: string}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: an admin token attempts to create a workspace

edge_cases.action: applies anti-enumeration — treats the workspace as not found
edge_cases.return_contract: returns HTTP 404 with JSON error body {error: string}
edge_cases.error_condition: a requester attempts any workspace operation on a workspace they do not own and are not admin

user_story.role: developer using a PAT with workspaces:write
user_story.goal: update mutable properties of a workspace via `PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/:slug`
user_story.benefit: workspace metadata can be maintained without requiring full owner credentials

acceptance_criteria.action: updates the provided fields (`display_name`, `description`, and/or `org_id`), sets `updated_at` to the current server time, and returns the full updated workspace object
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with JSON workspace object containing all fields including updated values and new `updated_at`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a valid PATCH request with at least one updatable field is received for an active workspace

acceptance_criteria.action: applies partial updates — fields absent from the PATCH body are left unchanged in the `workspaces table`
normalizes `display_name` to the workspace's `slug` value
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: response `display_name` equals the slug string
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `display_name` is set to null or empty string in the PATCH body

acceptance_criteria.action: normalizes `description` to empty string
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: response `description` equals empty string
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `description` is set to null or empty string in the PATCH body

acceptance_criteria.action: removes the organization association, setting `org_id` to NULL in the `workspaces table`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: response `org_id` is null
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `org_id` is set to null in the PATCH body

acceptance_criteria.action: updates `org_id` in the `workspaces table`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with updated workspace object containing the new `org_id`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the workspace owner is confirmed to be a member of that organization
acceptance_criteria.condition: `org_id` is set to a non-null value in the PATCH body

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects any attempt to change `slug`, `git_url`, `branch`, or `owner_id` via the PATCH body — these fields are immutable
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 400 with JSON error body {error: string} if immutable fields are included

edge_cases.return_contract: returns HTTP 400 with JSON error body {error: string}
edge_cases.error_condition: PATCH body is empty (no fields provided)

edge_cases.action: rejects the update — the workspace must be reactivated first
edge_cases.return_contract: returns HTTP 400 with JSON error body {error: string}
returns HTTP 400 with JSON error body {error: string}
edge_cases.error_condition: `display_name` in the PATCH body exceeds 128 characters

edge_cases.return_contract: returns HTTP 400 with JSON error body {error: string}
edge_cases.error_condition: `description` in the PATCH body exceeds 1024 characters

edge_cases.return_contract: returns HTTP 403 with JSON error body {error: string}
edge_cases.error_condition: `org_id` is set to a non-null value but the workspace owner is not a member of that organization

edge_cases.return_contract: returns HTTP 404 with JSON error body {error: string}
edge_cases.error_condition: the `slug` in the URL path does not match any workspace in the `workspaces table`

edge_cases.return_contract: returns HTTP 404 with JSON error body {error: string}
edge_cases.error_condition: the workspace exists but the requester does not own it and is not an admin

edge_cases.action: aborts the update without modifying the `workspaces table` and returns an error to the caller
edge_cases.return_contract: returns HTTP 500 with JSON error body {error: string}; no partial state is written
edge_cases.error_condition: the organization membership check call to an external service times out or returns an error status

requirements.title: Updated workspace response schema includes display_name and description

user_story.goal: receive `display_name` and `description` in every workspace API response
user_story.benefit: clients can display and use these fields without issuing a separate get request

acceptance_criteria.action: includes `display_name` and `description` as non-null string fields in the JSON response for create, list, get, update, archive, and reactivate operations
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: JSON object contains `display_name: string` and `description: string` — never null or absent

acceptance_criteria.action: includes all existing fields (`slug`, `git_url`, `branch`, `owner_id`, `org_id`, `status`, `created_at`, `updated_at`) in the response alongside the new fields
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: JSON object matches the documented response schema with all fields present

requirements.title: Updated POST /api/v1/workspaces accepts display_name and description

user_story.goal: pass `display_name` and `description` at creation time via `POST /api/v1/workspaces`
user_story.benefit: workspaces are fully described from the moment of creation without requiring a subsequent update call

acceptance_criteria.action: stores and returns those values, applying server-side defaults for any omitted or cleared fields
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 201 with JSON workspace object including the provided (or defaulted) `display_name` and `description`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `POST /api/v1/workspaces` receives optional `display_name` and/or `description` fields in the request body

acceptance_criteria.action: remains backward-compatible — requests that omit `display_name` and `description` continue to succeed and receive server-side defaults
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 201 with JSON workspace object; `display_name` equals slug, `description` equals empty string

user_story.goal: run `afc workspace update <slug>` to update `display_name`, `description`, or `org_id` of a workspace
user_story.benefit: workspace properties can be managed from the terminal without writing raw HTTP requests

acceptance_criteria.action: calls `PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/:slug` with the appropriate JSON body and prints the updated workspace object
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits 0; prints updated workspace JSON to stdout
acceptance_criteria.trigger: at least one of `--display-name`, `--description`, `--org`, `--clear-display-name`, `--clear-description`, or `--clear-org` is provided along with a valid `<slug>`

acceptance_criteria.action: maps `--clear-display-name` to sending `display_name: null` in the PATCH body, `--clear-description` to `description: null`, and `--clear-org` to `org_id: null`
only includes fields in the PATCH body for flags that were explicitly provided, leaving other fields absent

edge_cases.action: prints a usage hint to stderr and exits with code 1 without calling the API
edge_cases.return_contract: exits 1; usage hint on stderr; no HTTP request made

edge_cases.action: prints the error message from the JSON error body to stderr and exits with code 1
edge_cases.error_condition: the API returns a non-2xx HTTP status

edge_cases.action: prints an error message to stderr and exits with code 1; does not hang indefinitely
edge_cases.return_contract: exits 1 within the configured client timeout; error message on stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: the API call times out or the network connection fails

edge_cases.action: prints a descriptive error to stderr and exits with code 1
edge_cases.return_contract: exits 1; error message describing the parse failure on stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: the API returns a success status but the response body is missing or has unexpected shape

requirements.title: Updated afc workspace create CLI command with display_name and description flags

user_story.goal: pass `--display-name` and `--description` flags to `afc workspace create`
user_story.benefit: workspace metadata is set at creation without a separate update call

acceptance_criteria.action: includes those values in the `POST /api/v1/workspaces` request body
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits 0; prints created workspace JSON to stdout with the provided `display_name` and/or `description`

acceptance_criteria.action: remains backward-compatible when `--display-name` and `--description` flags are omitted — existing behavior and flags are unchanged
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits 0; prints created workspace JSON to stdout with server-side default `display_name` and `description`

user_story.goal: consult `docs/api.md` to understand all available REST endpoints, authentication requirements, request/response schemas, and error codes
user_story.benefit: developers can integrate correctly without reading source code

acceptance_criteria.action: covers all workspace endpoints: create, list, get, update, archive, reactivate, delete — including authentication requirements, request body schemas, response schemas, permission scope requirements, and HTTP error codes for each
documents the new `PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/:slug` endpoint with its partial-update semantics, field constraints, and all error conditions defined in this spec
documents the `workspaces:write` and `workspaces:delete` scopes, their implied permissions, and which endpoints they authorize
covers all non-workspace endpoints provided by `apikit` (login, user, keys, tokens, orgs, admin) at the same level of detail as workspace endpoints

user_story.goal: consult `docs/cli.md` for a complete reference of all `afc` commands, flags, and exit codes
user_story.benefit: operators can use the CLI correctly without trial and error

acceptance_criteria.action: covers all workspace subcommands: create, list, get, update, archive, reactivate, delete — including all flags, argument formats, and exit codes

documents the new `afc workspace update` subcommand with all flags (`--display-name`, `--description`, `--org`, `--clear-display-name`, `--clear-description`, `--clear-org`) and the exit-1 behavior when no flags are provided
covers all `apikit`-provided `afc` commands (login, user, keys, tokens, orgs, admin) at the same level of detail as workspace commands

correctness_properties.title: display_name is never null in responses
correctness_properties.for_any: workspace response returned by create, list, get, update, archive, or reactivate
correctness_properties.invariant: `display_name` is a non-null, non-empty string equal to either the user-supplied value or the workspace `slug`

correctness_properties.title: description is never null in responses
correctness_properties.for_any: workspace response returned by create, list, get, update, archive, or reactivate
correctness_properties.invariant: `description` is a non-null string (may be empty)

correctness_properties.title: Partial updates do not clobber unmentioned fields
correctness_properties.for_any: successful PATCH request that omits one or more of `display_name`, `description`, `org_id`
correctness_properties.invariant: the omitted fields in the `workspaces table` are identical before and after the update; only explicitly provided fields change

correctness_properties.title: updated_at advances on every successful update
correctness_properties.invariant: the `updated_at` value in the response is strictly greater than the `updated_at` value that existed before the request

correctness_properties.title: Immutable workspace fields are unchanged by update
correctness_properties.invariant: `slug`, `git_url`, `branch`, `owner_id`, `created_at`, and `status` in the `workspaces table` are identical before and after any update operation

correctness_properties.title: workspaces:delete does not grant list or get access
correctness_properties.invariant: list workspace and get workspace requests are denied with HTTP 404 (anti-enumeration)

correctness_properties.title: Existing scope behavior is unchanged
correctness_properties.invariant: the set of endpoints accessible and the responses returned are identical to those accessible before the introduction of `workspaces:write` and `workspaces:delete`

execution_paths.title: PAT with workspaces:write updates workspace display_name via CLI and API

steps.action: runs `afc workspace update my-project --display-name 'My Project'` with a `PAT` scoped to `workspaces:write`
validates that at least one flag is provided, then constructs a PATCH body `{"display_name": "My Project"}` and sends it to `PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/:slug`
authenticates the `PAT`, verifies it holds `workspaces:write`, and confirms the workspace is owned by the `PAT`'s user
validates the `display_name` length, confirms the workspace `status` is `active`, updates `display_name` and `updated_at` in the `workspaces table`
returns HTTP 200 with the full updated workspace JSON object
prints the updated workspace JSON to stdout and exits 0

execution_paths.title: PAT with workspaces:delete deletes an archived workspace

steps.action: calls `DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/old-project` with a `PAT` scoped to `workspaces:delete`, knowing the `slug` already
authenticates the `PAT`, verifies it holds `workspaces:delete`, and confirms the workspace is owned by the `PAT`'s user — does not require `workspaces:read`
confirms the workspace `status` is `archived`, then removes the row from the `workspaces table`

execution_paths.title: Workspace creation with display_name and description via CLI

steps.action: runs `afc workspace create --display-name 'My Project' --description 'A test workspace'` with a `PAT` holding `workspaces:create`
includes `display_name` and `description` in the `POST /api/v1/workspaces` request body
validates field lengths, stores `display_name` and `description` alongside the other workspace fields in the `workspaces table`
returns HTTP 201 with the full workspace JSON including `display_name` and `description`
prints the created workspace JSON to stdout and exits 0

execution_paths.title: Update endpoint rejects archived workspace — full request-to-error path

steps.action: sends `PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/archived-ws` with a valid owner API key and body `{"description": "new text"}`
authenticates the request and confirms ownership
retrieves the workspace from the `workspaces table`, detects `status` = `archived`, and aborts without writing any changes
returns HTTP 400 with JSON error body indicating the workspace must be reactivated before updating

error_handling.condition: PATCH body is empty (no fields provided)
error_handling.behavior: returns HTTP 400 with JSON error body {error: string}; no database write is performed

error_handling.condition: target workspace has status = archived when PATCH is called
error_handling.behavior: returns HTTP 400 with JSON error body {error: string}; update is rejected without any modification
returns HTTP 400 with JSON error body {error: string}; no database write is performed
returns HTTP 400 with JSON error body {error: string}; no database write is performed

error_handling.condition: workspace owner is not a member of the org specified in `org_id`
error_handling.behavior: returns HTTP 403 with JSON error body {error: string}; `org_id` is not updated

error_handling.condition: `slug` in URL path does not correspond to any workspace
error_handling.behavior: returns HTTP 404 with JSON error body {error: string}

error_handling.condition: workspace exists but requester does not own it and is not admin
error_handling.behavior: returns HTTP 404 (anti-enumeration) with JSON error body {error: string}; resource existence is not disclosed

error_handling.condition: organization membership check times out or returns an error status from the external service
error_handling.behavior: returns HTTP 500 with JSON error body {error: string}; no partial state is written to the `workspaces table`

error_handling.condition: `afc workspace update` is invoked with no update flags
error_handling.behavior: prints usage hint to stderr and exits with code 1; no HTTP request is made

error_handling.condition: `afc workspace update` receives a non-2xx HTTP response from the API
error_handling.behavior: prints the error message from the JSON error body to stderr and exits with code 1

error_handling.condition: `afc workspace update` call times out or network connection fails
error_handling.behavior: exits 1 within the configured client timeout; prints timeout or connection error to stderr

error_handling.condition: `afc workspace update` receives a success HTTP status but response body is malformed or missing expected fields
error_handling.behavior: prints a descriptive parse error to stderr and exits with code 1
returns HTTP 400 with JSON error body {error: string}; workspace is not created
returns HTTP 400 with JSON error body {error: string}; workspace is not created

error_handling.condition: admin token attempts to create a workspace
error_handling.behavior: returns HTTP 403 with JSON error body {error: string}; workspace creation requires a real user as owner

external_apis.version: unverified — cross-check installed version before implementation

symbols.name: Permission
symbols.signature: apikit.Permission (type or function used to register a named PAT permission scope in MountHandlers)
symbols.notes: Exact signature unverified; PRD states this is the same mechanism used for existing workspaces:read and workspaces:create scopes. Verify actual signature against installed library.
